title: Trust
description: A trust instrument — testamentary or inter vivos. Covers discretionary, bare, life interest, charitable, nil rate band, waqf (Islamic endowment), and offshore structures with governance features (reserved powers, flee clauses, protector powers)

id.description: Unique identifier for this trust within the INHERIT document

name.description: Name of the trust, as it appears in the trust instrument

trustType.$comment: discretionary: trustees have full discretion over distributions — most flexible, used for IHT planning (UK). life_interest: income/use to one person for life, then capital passes to remainderman. bare: beneficiary has absolute right to trust property — often for minors until age 18. accumulation_and_maintenance: accumulates income for minors until they reach a specified age. disabled_persons: trust for a disabled person — special IHT treatment. charitable: trust established for charitable purposes — exempt from IHT and CGT. nil_rate_band: UK-specific — absorbs the nil rate band for IHT purposes. waqf: Islamic endowment — perpetual, irrevocable, income for charitable or family purposes. other: any trust type not covered above.

trustees.description: Persons appointed as trustees, protectors, or enforcers of this trust

beneficiaries.description: Beneficiaries of the trust — named individuals, classes, or organisations

settlor.description: Reference to the Person.id of the settlor (the person who created and funded the trust)

isTestamentary.description: Whether the trust is created by the will (testamentary) or was established during the settlor's lifetime (inter vivos)

createdDate.description: Date the trust instrument was executed

creationType.$comment: testamentary: created by the will, takes effect on death. inter_vivos_revocable: created during lifetime, can be revoked by the settlor (common in US estate planning). inter_vivos_irrevocable: created during lifetime, cannot be revoked.

revocability.description: Whether the trust can be revoked, and whether it is perpetual
revocability.$comment: revocable: can be revoked by the settlor. irrevocable: cannot be revoked — common for asset protection. perpetual: no end date — waqf and certain Cayman STAR trusts. Distinct from creationType. A trust created as inter_vivos_revocable may become irrevocable on the settlor's death.

perpetual.description: Whether the trust is perpetual (has no fixed end date). Waqf endowments and certain offshore trusts are perpetual by nature
perpetual.$comment: True for waqf and certain offshore perpetual trusts.

vestingAge.description: Age at which the beneficiary's interest vests absolutely (for A&M trusts and bare trusts for minors)

vestingDate.description: Fixed date on which the trust vests, if not age-based

vestingConditions.description: Conditions that must be met for the trust to vest
examples: Beneficiary must have graduated from university
Beneficiary must be in stable employment

contingentInterests.description: Interests in the trust that are contingent on future events
examples: If the life tenant dies without issue, capital passes to the charity
On the youngest child reaching 25, the trust winds up

trustPeriod.description: The trust period or perpetuity period, if applicable. In England and Wales, the statutory perpetuity period is 125 years (Perpetuities and Accumulations Act 2009)

governingLaw.description: The jurisdiction whose law governs this trust

reservedPowers.description: Powers reserved by the settlor or granted to a third party. Offshore trusts frequently include reserved powers over investment or distributions
reservedPowers.$comment: Powers retained by the settlor or granted to a third party. Common in BVI VISTA trusts, Jersey trusts, and Cayman STAR trusts.

powerType.$comment: investment: power to direct or veto investment decisions. distribution: power to direct distributions. amendment: power to amend trust terms. revocation: power to revoke the trust. addition_of_beneficiaries: power to add new beneficiaries. removal_of_trustees: power to remove and appoint trustees. change_of_governing_law: power to change the trust's governing jurisdiction.

heldByPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id who holds this power

conditions.description: Any conditions on the exercise of this power
examples: May only be exercised with protector consent

fleeClause.description: A flee clause allowing automatic or discretionary change of trust situs when triggering events occur. Standard in offshore trust planning
fleeClause.$comment: Clause allowing automatic change of trust situs if triggering events occur (e.g., change of law, political instability). Standard in offshore trusts.

present.description: Whether the trust contains a flee clause

triggerEvents.description: Events that trigger the flee clause
examples: Change of law adverse to trust beneficiaries

destinationJurisdiction.description: The jurisdiction the trust flees to when triggered

automaticOrDiscretionary.description: Whether the flee is triggered automatically or at trustee discretion
automaticOrDiscretionary.$comment: automatic: trust moves automatically on trigger event. discretionary: trustee decides whether to move.

protectorPowers.description: Powers held by the trust protector or enforcer. Offshore trusts commonly use protectors to oversee trustees
protectorPowers.$comment: Specific powers held by the trust protector. Cayman STAR trusts REQUIRE an enforcer. Jersey/Guernsey trusts commonly have protectors.

powerType.description: The type of protector or enforcer power
powerType.$comment: consent_to_distribution: trustee must obtain protector consent before distributing. remove_trustee: power to remove a trustee. appoint_trustee: power to appoint a replacement trustee. change_governing_law: power to change the trust's governing law. veto_investment: power to veto investment decisions. add_beneficiary: power to add persons to the beneficiary class. exclude_beneficiary: power to remove persons from the beneficiary class. enforce_purpose: enforce the trust's stated purpose (Cayman STAR enforcer role).

protectorPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id who holds this power

exerciseConditions.description: Conditions on how this power may be exercised
examples: Must act in the best interests of the beneficiaries

jurisdictionalRecognition.description: How different jurisdictions recognise (or fail to recognise) this trust. Critical for cross-border trust structures
jurisdictionalRecognition.$comment: How different jurisdictions recognise this trust. Critical for offshore trusts used by HK/Singapore/Geneva clients.

jurisdiction.description: The jurisdiction whose recognition status is recorded

recognised.description: Whether this jurisdiction recognises the trust

recognisedAs.description: How the jurisdiction characterises the trust for legal and tax purposes

hagueTrustConventionApplies.description: Whether the Hague Trust Convention applies in this jurisdiction — determines automatic recognition of foreign trusts

notes.description: Additional context on recognition in this jurisdiction
examples: France treats as a fiducie — different tax implications

assetProtectionFeatures.description: Asset protection features of the trust. Relevant for Cook Islands, Nevis, BVI, and domestic US asset protection trusts (Delaware, Nevada, South Dakota)
assetProtectionFeatures.$comment: For Cook Islands, Nevis, BVI, and domestic US asset protection trusts.

selfSettled.description: Whether the settlor is also a beneficiary. Self-settled trusts face greater creditor vulnerability in most jurisdictions
selfSettled.$comment: True if the settlor is also a beneficiary. Affects creditor claims.

spendthriftClause.description: Whether the trust contains a spendthrift clause preventing beneficiaries from assigning their interest

fraudulentTransferLookback.description: The statutory lookback period for fraudulent transfer claims in the trust's jurisdiction
fraudulentTransferLookback.$comment: Lookback period for fraudulent transfer claims (e.g., '2 years' for Cook Islands, '6 years' for BVI).

domesticationDate.description: Date the trust was domesticated (moved from offshore to onshore jurisdiction), if applicable
domesticationDate.$comment: If an offshore trust was domesticated (moved onshore).

notes.description: Free-text notes about this trust. Use for trustee succession plans, special investment mandates, or other non-structured information
examples: Trust was established to hold the family's BVI property portfolio
Waqf income designated for educational scholarships in perpetuity

TrustAppointee.description: A person appointed to a governance role in the trust — trustee, protector, or enforcer

personId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the appointee

role.description: The governance role this person holds in the trust
role.$comment: trustee: manages trust assets and administers the trust. protector: oversees trustees and may have consent or veto powers. enforcer: enforces purpose trust terms — required for Cayman STAR trusts.

TrustBeneficiary.description: A beneficiary of the trust — may be a named individual, a class, or an organisation

personId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the beneficiary, if a named individual

classDefinition.description: Natural-language description of the beneficiary class
examples: All my grandchildren and their descendants
Any person the trustees may add to the class

interestType.description: The nature of this beneficiary's interest in the trust
interestType.$comment: income: entitled to income generated by trust assets only. capital: entitled to capital distributions only. both: entitled to both income and capital. discretionary: trustees have discretion over what and when to distribute.
